purpose: Critical electrical interface component providing stable electrical connections to heating elements with minimal signal drift and contact resistance

states.name: zero_adjustment_accuracy
states.description: Zero offset accuracy degradation due to thermal effects and contamination

states.name: contact_resistance
states.description: Electrical contact resistance affecting signal integrity

events.name: startup_voltage_jump
events.description: Output signal jumps to +4...5V when power first applied

events.name: inadequate_stabilization
events.description: Signal not stable within millivolts close to zero after warm-up

parameters.name: zero_drift_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for zero adjustment accuracy degradation

parameters.name: thermal_stress_factor
parameters.description: Thermal stress multiplier affecting zero drift rate

parameters.name: resistance_growth_rate
parameters.description: Rate of contact resistance increase due to oxidation and wear

parameters.name: max_contact_resistance
parameters.description: Maximum allowable contact resistance before failure

parameters.name: zero_accuracy_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum zero adjustment accuracy for proper operation

parameters.name: startup_threshold
parameters.description: Zero accuracy threshold below which startup voltage jumps occur

parameters.name: stabilization_resistance_threshold
parameters.description: Contact resistance threshold above which stabilization is inadequate

state_transition_logic: zero_adjustment_accuracy decreases by (1.0 / zero_drift_timescale) * thermal_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
contact_resistance increases by resistance_growth_rate * dt, clamped to [0.01, max_contact_resistance]
event_logic: inadequate_stabilization occurs when zero_adjustment_accuracy < zero_accuracy_threshold OR contact_resistance > stabilization_resistance_threshold
assumptions: Thermal stress factor remains constant during operation
Contact resistance increases monotonically due to oxidation and mechanical wear
Zero adjustment accuracy degrades primarily due to thermal cycling and contamination
Startup voltage jumps are primarily caused by zero offset drift
Signal stabilization depends on both zero accuracy and contact resistance
